question: What is the definition of the acronym LOCA?

nuscale_ch01_0720.title: DSRS 15.2.6, Rev 0: Loss of nullmergency AC Power to the Station AuxiliariesII.5 Most Limiting Plant System Single Failure
nuscale_ch01_0720.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for various accident scenarios, including loss of nonemergency AC power and feedwater system failures. For each scenario, the report confirms compliance with relevant safety requirements, analytical methods, and regulatory guidance, noting any specific categorizations or conservative assumptions used. No exceptions or non-conformances are identified in the reviewed sections.

nuscale_ch01_0780.title: SRP 15.6.5.A, Rev 1: Radiological Consequences of a Design Basis Loss-of-Coolant Accident Including Containment Leakage ContributionII.1 Calculated Doses and Containment Leakage Contribution
nuscale_ch01_0780.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for evaluating radiological consequences of a design basis loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA), including containment leakage. It notes that while NuScale’s analysis methods generally conform or partially conform to the relevant SRP guidance, certain acceptance criteria and analysis models are superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3, and some guidance applies only to BWRs.

nuscale_ch01_0725.title: DSRS 15.2.8, Rev 0: Feedwater System Pipe Break Inside and Outside Containment (PWR)II.1 Reactor Coolant System and Main Steam System Pressures
nuscale_ch01_0725.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for DSRS 15.2.8, addressing feedwater system pipe breaks inside and outside containment for PWRs. It confirms that NuScale meets requirements for reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, core damage potential (using heat generation rate limits for slower reactivity insertions), and calculated site boundary doses, with no exceptions noted.

nuscale_ch01_0779.title: DSRS 15.6.5, Rev 0: Loss-of-Coolant Accidents Resulting from Spectrum of Postulated Piping Breaks within the Reactor Coolant Pressure BoundaryII.4 Programmatic Requirements
nuscale_ch01_0779.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for loss-of-coolant accidents (LOCA) as outlined in DSRS 15.6.5 and related SRP sections. It notes full or partial conformance with requirements for calculating radiological consequences and containment leakage, with some criteria superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3 and certain guidance applicable only to BWRs. The table documents how NuScale addresses each regulatory requirement.

edges.description: Section B explicitly defines and discusses Loss-of-Coolant Accidents (LOCA), which is the acronym in question, while Section A does not mention or define LOCA, thus Section B provides the definition sought in the analysis question.

Section A references the term 'Loss-of-Coolant Accident' (LOCA) as part of its title and analysis, while Section B provides the full phrase and context for the acronym LOCA, thus Section B defines the term used in Section A.
